channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized
item.description:
As we have done for more than a decade, the Rio Grande Foundation has tracked the votes in New Mexico’s legislative sessions. The 2026 session was a short, 30-day legislative session, but some momentous bills passed (for better AND worse) and some horrible bills that could have passed were defeated, but often included floor votes […]

The post RGF Freedom Index Results Finalized appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Libertas] Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network
item.description:
Libertas Network is growing. As we expand our national programs to serve millions of families and spread the ideas of human flourishing, we need leaders who match the scale of that ambition. That’s why we’re excited to announce the addition of three extraordinary individuals to our board of trustees. Each one brings deep experience, bold […]

The post Three New Board Members Join Libertas Network appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Arkansaspolicyfoundation] SOLAR, WIND & ARKANSAS MOTOR VEHICLES
item.description: (February 2026) Arkansans are heavily dependent on private transportation. The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) reports that 93% of Arkansans reach work by private motor vehicles, while only 0.4% use public transportation. The 102,622 miles of roads in Arkansas dwarf the 2,505 miles of freight railroad and 1,860 miles of waterway cited by the BTS, […]

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Job Opening: Donor Relations and Events Manager
item.description:
Donor Relations and Events Manager Maine Policy Institute (MPI) is seeking an energetic, organized, detail-oriented, full-time development professional to assist our director of operations.  This position will be responsible for accurate donor and database management, including timely donor follow-up and programmatic updates, event planning that engages and educates diverse audiences, coordinating an effective direct mail […]

The post Job Opening: Donor Relations and Events Manager appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] Why Tennessee Doesn’t Need a State Property Tax
item.description: We’ve made it just fine for more than 75 years without a state property tax, and we’ll be just fine in the future without one.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed
item.description:
The following appeared in the Albuquerque Journal on March 2nd, 2026 and in other newspapers across New Mexico. The passage of medical malpractice reform is the most important public policy success in New Mexico in more than a decade. It took support from New Mexicans of every political stripe and a bipartisan coalition in the Legislature to […]

The post Opinion piece: RGF opinion piece: Medical malpractice success a win, but more needed appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals
item.description:
Alabama’s rural hospitals are struggling, and our state’s bureaucratic red tape isn’t helping. The Alabama Hospital Association reports 27 hospitals in danger of closing and 19 in immediate risk.

The post Alabama Needs to Cut the Red Tape for Rural Hospitals appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies
item.description:
One of the most powerful political coalitions ever assembled in state history was launched today, bringing the state’s most influential industry and advocacy groups together with a singular purpose: to go on offense against policies that will raise costs for families and employers in Alabama.

The post Alabama’s most influential political groups converge as ‘Freedom First Alabama’ to wage war on big government policies appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers
item.description:
Yesterday marked one of the most important procedural deadlines of the legislative session. It was the deadline for committees to report general bills and constitutional amendments originating in the other chamber. While it may sound technical, this deadline has a very real impact on which policies still have a chance of becoming law this year. Here’s […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: A Key Legislative Deadline — and a Bill to Help Mississippi’s Independent Workers appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Alabamapolicy] Stephanie Smith: The $100 million myth vs. Alabama’s $12 billion reality
item.description:
Last week, during a "State of the Schools" event in North Alabama, leaders from Huntsville City, Madison City, and Madison County schools sounded a familiar but mathematically creative alarm.

The post Stephanie Smith: The $100 million myth vs. Alabama’s $12 billion reality appeared first on Alabama Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Building Affordability: The Role of Single-Family Rentals in Expanding Housing Supply
item.description:
The housing affordability crisis is an issue being faced throughout the country.1This has caused frustration for hardworking Americans,...

The post Building Affordability: The Role of Single-Family Rentals in Expanding Housing Supply  appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs.
item.description:
Politicians in Augusta look poised to pass LDs 229 and 1089 this session, which would raise Maine’s top income tax rate to 10.95%. However, our research concludes that either one of these bills could individually cause serious damage to Maine’s economy if passed. If both bills were passed, it would stack multiple tax increases on […]

The post LDs 229 + 1089: Higher Taxes. Slower Growth. Fewer Jobs. appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida
item.description:
Introduction Florida’s current property tax system places a growing financial burden on homeowners, renters, and employers across the...

The post Levy Caps for Property Tax Reform in Florida appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH could be the first state to let non-union public employees negotiate their contracts
item.description:
New Hampshire led the American colonies 250 years ago by adopt a constitution that created an independent government before anyone else. So it’s fitting that in 2026 New Hampshire could achieve another first for independence. Like all Americans, government employees have the constitutional right to free speech and free association. In 2018, the U.S. Supreme […]

The post NH could be the first state to let non-union public employees negotiate their contracts appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities
item.description:
Georgia lawmakers have drafted a pair of bills that are set to transform the way math is taught in the state. If the bills become law, they’ll open doors for students to receive a high-quality math education and ultimately shape the direction of their lives.

The post Unleashing Students’ Potential: Georgia Legislators Champion Advanced Math Opportunities appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Kansas leads the world in ACT score declines
item.description: The Kansas Department of Education’s mantra – Kansas leads the world in the success of every student – would be more accurately stated as Kansas leads the world in ACT score declines. District-level ACT scores for 2025 were finally made public yesterday, and it’s no surprise that KSDE kept them hidden for months. The state […]

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Louisiana Supreme Court Delivers a Win for Common Sense and Economic Growth
item.description: What if a single court decision could unlock billions in investment, protect local decision-making, and secure Louisiana’s role as an energy powerhouse? That is exactly what happened when the Louisiana Supreme Court overturned an appellate ruling and affirmed St. James Parish’s authority to approve Koch Methanol’s plant expansion. This victory is not just a legal milestone. It...

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] In talent squeeze, independent schools respond — and seek relief
item.description:
Facing a difficult market for hiring teaching talent, leaders of Wisconsin independent schools are developing useful strategies even as they look to the state for fundamental policy and financial reforms.

The post In talent squeeze, independent schools respond — and seek relief appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Report: “A Review of Metro’s Regional Passenger Rail Futures Study”
item.description:
By Randal O’Toole March 2026 Executive Summary In 2024, the Oregon legislature Senate...

The post Report: “A Review of Metro’s Regional Passenger Rail Futures Study” appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional
item.description:
Last month, in our Open Letter to Maine’s Legislature, we urged lawmakers to stop advancing LD 1666, the bill that would force ranked-choice voting (RCV) on elections for Governor, State Senator, and State Representative. We reminded lawmakers of the Maine Constitution’s clear plurality requirement, the Maine Supreme Judicial Court’s unanimous 2017 advisory opinion declaring RCV […]

The post Even Maine’s Democratic Attorney General Argues Ranked-Choice Voting is Unconstitutional appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Pelican Institute Releases New Report Exposing $115 Million in Outside Funding and Outdated Policies Blocking Louisiana Energy Dominance
item.description: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Media Contact: sydney@pelicaninstitute.org March 13, 2026 New Orleans, LA— The Pelican Institute for Public Policy today released Barriers to Louisiana Energy Dominance, a comprehensive white paper examining the legislative, regulatory, and public-discourse obstacles preventing Louisiana from realizing its full energy potential. Louisiana possesses the natural resources and geographic positioning to lead the nation,...

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Let’s Clear the Air: Separating FACT from FICTION on LA GATOR
item.description: There’s been a lot of chatter lately about Louisiana’s Giving All True Opportunity to Rise (LA GATOR) education option. Questions are good—state policymakers and the people of Louisiana deserve answers and access to the information they need—but debate should remain focused on fact, not fiction. Let’s cut through the noise with facts drawn from the...

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 10
item.description:
Capitol Chat: Week 10 At the Mississippi Capitol, nothing is truly dead until it’s dead, dead, dead. This week’s episode features the resurrection of teacher pay raises, movement on pharmacy benefit manager reform, and the latest on a controversial NIL tax proposal.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 10 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Beacontn] Fact or Cap? Property Tax Caps in Tennessee
item.description: With so much media coverage and discussion around a property tax cap, what is the reality of such a policy, and is one needed in Tennessee?

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Cascade’s Testimony on Metro Spending $50M, 40 Years, Turning Superfund Site to a Park
item.description:
By Naomi Inman Testimony by John A. Charles, Jr. This March, the Metro...

The post Cascade’s Testimony on Metro Spending $50M, 40 Years, Turning Superfund Site to a Park appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Modernizing CPA Licensure
item.description:
Policy takeaway:  Nebraska’s newly passed LB718 modernizes CPA licensure by expanding pathways into the profession while preserving rigorous education, examination, and experience standards. The law strengthens Nebraska’s accounting workforce pipeline and aligns the state with updated national mobility rules that allow CPAs to serve clients across state lines.  Nebraska has taken an important step to...

The post Modernizing CPA Licensure appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story
item.description:
RGF has been very active in opposing the plan to enact a massive gross receipts tax hike at Albuquerque City Council. You can find out more about the plan here and even use the links in the post to reach out to your council members. RGF president Paul Gessing recently sat down with KOAT 7 […]

The post RGF weighs in against proposed Albuquerque gross receipts tax hike in KOAT 7 story appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Who Moved in 2025? Nebraska’s Window of Opportunity
item.description:
Every year, the “National Movers Study” from United Van Lines and the “U-Haul Growth Index” provide a real-time heat map of the American economy. The 2025 results are in, and they deliver a clear message: while the mass exodus from high-tax coastal states continues, the “quiet competition” for the middle of the country is heating...

The post Who Moved in 2025? Nebraska’s Window of Opportunity appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Spring Report
item.description:
IRG Spring Report_May 2026

The post IRG Spring Report appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Sunshine Week 2026
item.description:
Sunshine Week is an opportunity to celebrate transparency in government. Unfortunately, this year we once again observe it by pointing out major shortcomings in open government—starting with the Massachusetts Legislature’s continued refusal to practice it. Voters spoke clearly in 2024: they want the...

The post Sunshine Week 2026 appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: Gen. Wayne: An ‘Acceptable’ War “Whoever has a sword should take it.” (Luke 22:36) SOME OF US acknowledge the concept of a “just war” while realizing that it becomes mere semantics when swords clash and gunpowder ignites. The destruction of Iran’s Islamic regime would fit the definition, though. As would the arrest of Nicolás Maduro and the Read the full article...

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation Appeals Dismissal of Lawsuit Challenging Oregon ‘Anti-Impersonation’ Law
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today appealed to the 9th Circuit Court of Appeals after a district court judge last week dismissed its case challenging Oregon HB 3789.

The post Freedom Foundation Appeals Dismissal of Lawsuit Challenging Oregon ‘Anti-Impersonation’ Law appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

What Wisconsin Wants_The Views of 2024 Trump Voters Who Sat Out the 2022 Gubernatorial Election

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] INTERVIEW: NJPI Chairman Discusses the NJ Education Landscape on New Jersey Politics with Laura Jones
item.description:
Check out NJPI Chairman Andy Mulvihill as he is interviewed by Laura Jones on the landscape of K-12 education in […]

item.title: [Empowerms] Rep. Sam Creekmore: Chasing Lions in Healthcare
item.description:
Rep. Sam Creekmore: Chasing Lions in Healthcare In this episode of The Empower Podcast, host Grant Callen sits down with State Representative Sam Creekmore, chairman of the House Public Health and Human Services Committee, for a wide-ranging conversation about the major healthcare issues facing Mississippi. From certificate of need reform and rural hospital access to…

The post Rep. Sam Creekmore: Chasing Lions in Healthcare first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] How to keep good teachers in the classroom
item.description:
Nine policy recommendations for Wisconsin schools to keep teaching talent by correcting the conditions that lead to teacher loss.

The post How to keep good teachers in the classroom appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] The Data Center Panic Isn’t Backed by Data
item.description: Data centers may not be the typical industrial building, like a Nissan, General Motors, or Volkswagen plant, yet they are less of a boogey man than they are made out to be.

item.title: [Will Law] Voters Given Opportunity to Reject Costly 400-Year Veto
item.description:
WILL issued a statement applauding the Wisconsin State Senate for passing Senate Joint Resolution 116. This proposed amendment to the Wisconsin Constitution curbs executive overreach by prohibiting the governor from using the partial veto to create or increase taxes or fees. Voters will have to ratify it this November for it to take effect.

The post Voters Given Opportunity to Reject Costly 400-Year Veto appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] The 2027 Responsible Kansas Budget
item.description: Kansas is relearning a lesson it should already know: the problem is not revenue. The problem is spending discipline. That is the real issue behind the current budget debate. The House budget for fiscal year 2027 would spend nearly $27 billion in total funds and almost $11 billion from the State General Fund, while the […]

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Case for a Special Session
item.description:
Governor Tate Reeves announced that he is open to calling a special legislative session on education. He should. Two and a half months into the session, Mississippi’s state legislature has so far accomplished remarkably little. Senator Jeremy England’s well thought out efforts to restore the ballot initiative (SCR 518) failed. Speaker Jason White and Representative […]

The post The Case for a Special Session appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Inpolicy] Varvel: John Maynard Keynes
item.description: by GARY VARVEL  The First Commandment is clear: “You shall have no other gods before Me” (Exodus 20:3). Obedience to this principle is the foundation of a stable society; conversely, replacing God with any earthly power is a form of idolatry doomed to ultimate failure. While the first five men in this series rejected the God Read the full article...

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] MINNESOTA: Workers heading for the exits
item.description:
For six straight months, more than 100 Minnesota public employees a month have cut ties with their unions.

The post MINNESOTA: Workers heading for the exits appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Empirecenter] Ideas for Cleaning Up New York Medicaid
item.description:
As the Trump administration cracks down on fraud, waste and abuse in Medicaid, New York is a logical place to....

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome Senator Tom Brandt of District 32 and Senator Bob Hallstrom of District 1. In this episode, the senators join Platte Institute CEO Jim Vokal to discuss their efforts to address Nebraska’s property tax burden through LB 1219 and LB 575 (now amended into LB...

The post Nebraskanomics: Senators Brandt and Hallstrom on Property Tax Reform this Year appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Dispatch from the Dome: Reform, Regulation, and What Comes Next at the Capitol
item.description:
As the legislative session moves into its final stretch, the focus at the Capitol is beginning to shift. Major policy bills are clearing their final hurdles, others have quietly fallen away, and attention is now turning toward the biggest issue left unresolved: the state budget. Here’s a look at what moved last week — and […]

The post Dispatch from the Dome: Reform, Regulation, and What Comes Next at the Capitol appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Florida Education Association Ordered to Pay Teacher’s Legal Fees After Unfair Labor Practice Ruling
item.description:
Florida’s Public Employees Relations Commission (PERC) has ruled against the Florida Education Association in an unfair labor practice case, ordering the union to cover attorney fees and litigation costs for retired Martin County teacher Kelley LaBedz. Florida’s Public Employees Relations Commission (PERC) has ruled against the Florida Education Association in an unfair labor practice case, ordering the union to cover attorney fees and litigation costs for retired Martin County teacher Kelley LaBedz.

The post Florida Education Association Ordered to Pay Teacher’s Legal Fees After Unfair Labor Practice Ruling  appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Manchester Transit Authority spending surged as ridership fell over the last dozen years
item.description:
The Manchester Transit Authority (MTA) has dramatically expanded its bus routes and coverage area over the last dozen years, pushing operating costs higher. Yet as costs and coverage soared, ridership sank. As the mayor and aldermen search for savings in a stressed city budget, the MTA is an agency that could be scrutinized for possible […]

The post Manchester Transit Authority spending surged as ridership fell over the last dozen years appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Jbartlett] First a paint tax, now a battery tax
item.description:
Gov. Kelly Ayotte on Monday vetoed House Bill 451, which would have established a statewide paint recycling program funded by an “assessment” on paint.  Merriam-Webster notes that for more than 500 years the word “assessment” has meant “to determine the rate or amount of (a tax).” Gov. Ayotte correctly called the assessment a tax, and […]

The post First a paint tax, now a battery tax appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] NEW: WHY THE WISCONSIN SUPREME COURT MATTERS
item.description:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE March 19, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org NEW: WHY THE WISCONSIN SUPREME COURT MATTERS  Delafield, WI — The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG) announced the release of a comprehensive Court Watch Spring Report on Thursday, bringing together twelve research briefs examining how the Wisconsin Supreme Court shapes the state’s government, economy, and civil society. […]

The post NEW: WHY THE WISCONSIN SUPREME COURT MATTERS  appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Exposes Race-Based Highway Construction Schemes in Four States
item.description:
WILL filed a new Title VI civil rights complaint with the U.S. Department of Transportation (USDOT), identifying four states that are flouting the Trump Administration’s directive to end DEI in highway construction.

The post WILL Exposes Race-Based Highway Construction Schemes in Four States appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Tax Flight Takes Off: IRS Data Reveal Surge of Massachusetts Residents Fleeing to Tax-Friendly States Following the 4 Percent Surtax
item.description:
Net AGI Loss to Florida and New Hampshire Quintuples in a Decade as 26-35-Year-Old Outmigration Continues Newly released 2023 IRS migration data – the first full year after the 4 percent surtax on incomes over $1 million took effect – show Massachusetts losing more than double the amount of adjusted gross income...

The post Tax Flight Takes Off: IRS Data Reveal Surge of Massachusetts Residents Fleeing to Tax-Friendly States Following the 4 Percent Surtax appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.description:
The University of Wisconsin System this month opened the door for its campuses to begin offering faster, cheaper three-year degrees.

item.title: [Foropportunity] Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill
item.description:
State legislators are paving the way for children to overcome challenges with literacy, empowering them to thrive in their academic development and build flourishing lives for themselves and their families in the future.

The post Stronger Readers, Brighter Futures: Georgia Lawmakers Open Doors with New Literacy Bill appeared first on Georgia Center For Opportunity.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Idaho now has 13,568 reasons to consider expanding parental choice tax credit
item.description: As Idaho’s application window for the Parental Choice Tax Credit has come to a close, the final numbers tell a powerful story. We now know the final numbers submitted to the state tax commission by the the March 15 deadline: 6,069 families applied, covering 13,568 students across the state.

item.title: [Inpolicy] The Outstater
item.description: The Political Distraction of Youth THERE IS SOMETHING about the middle-aged mind that imagines it can transmit seamlessly to the next generation the observations, wisdom and judgment of a lifetime. The reality, for better or for worse, is that each generation makes up its own mind in its own time. Nonetheless, we continue to launch grand projects in Read the full article...

Herbert M. “Tim” Richards, III, Vice Chair      Senate Committee on Housing      Sen. Stanley Chang, Chair      Sen. Troy N. Hashimoto, Vice […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Florida’s Private Sector Is Booming
item.description:
When economists assess a state’s economic health, private sector employment is one of the clearest signals of momentum. It’s a lagging economic indicator that corresponds to increases in business creation, wage growth, and consumer activity, so when private sector employment is growing robustly, it bodes well for a state’s overall economic outlook and health.  In recent years, Florida has stood out as one of the...

The post Florida’s Private Sector Is Booming  appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Empowerms] 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 11
item.description:
﻿ Capitol Chat: Week 11 With only a few weeks remaining in the 2026 legislative session, lawmakers are narrowing their focus. This week, we take a look at three issues dominating discussions at the Capitol and what’s on the horizon as the 2026 session winds down.

The post 2026 Capitol Chat: Week 11 first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Week in Review: A Regulatory Rein-In
item.description:
The power to make laws should rest with the people and their elected representatives—not government bureaucrats who impose costly rules..

The post Week in Review: A Regulatory Rein-In appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL: Evers Veto of “Red Tape Reset” is Irresponsible
item.description:
Governor Tony Evers vetoed the WILL-backed “Red Tape Reset,” a comprehensive regulatory reform package supported by a broad coalition of free-market groups and Wisconsin job creators.

The post WILL: Evers Veto of “Red Tape Reset” is Irresponsible appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Understanding the New Federal Tax Credit for K–12 Scholarships: Will California Opt In?
item.description:
Last year, the federal government passed a law that created a tax credit for contributions to Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs). SGOs are nonprofits that fund student educational opportunities through scholarships. If California opts in, students in the state would benefit from expanded scholarship programs, all without needing to spend any state or local government dollars....

The post Understanding the New Federal Tax Credit for K–12 Scholarships: Will California Opt In? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] LAUSD is broke, and the union knows it
item.description:
After Los Angeles Unified delivered 3,200 pink slips to teachers and employees last month, the United Teachers Los Angeles has the temerity to strike for higher pay. As one of the most fiscally distressed school districts in America, drowning in an $11.4 billion unrestricted net deficit, hemorrhaging students, and still without its own superintendent due to ongoing...

The post LAUSD is broke, and the union knows it first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Rollback Stabilizes Property Taxes
item.description:
What rollback does: Iowa’s rollback limits how much of a property’s value is taxed, ensuring taxable values grow more slowly than market values, helping prevent sudden spikes in property tax bills. How it works: The rollback is recalculated each year for residential and agricultural property to cap statewide taxable value growth, adjusting automatically as valuations rise or fall. Why it matters: Rollback doesn’t control government spending, but it protects taxpayers by stabilizing the tax base, so rising property values don’t automatically translate into sharply higher tax bills.

The post Rollback Stabilizes Property Taxes appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Let’s fix licensing systems that lock ex-offenders out of work
item.description: There is nothing right about killing careers for those who have paid their debts to society

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Tax concern should be a warning to Chicago’s mayor
item.description:
Almost half of those in a new poll named taxes one of the city top-two issues. Concern about the economy is on the rise.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] IRS domestic migration data shows another net loss for Kansas and Johnson County
item.description: The 2023 domestic migration data released last week by the Internal Revenue Service shows that Kansas lost $361 million in adjusted gross income (AGI) from U.S. residents moving in and out of the state. That is a smaller loss than in 2022, but more than was lost in 2020 and 2021. Johnson County elected officials […]

item.title: [Texaspolicy] Study: K-12 Grade Inflation Making American Kids Dumber, Costing Them Millions
item.description:
When teachers inflate grades — giving higher marks than students’ actual performance warrants — the effects ripple far beyond the classroom. Increasingly widespread high school grade inflation can cost students more than $200,000 in lost earning potential per teacher per year, finds a groundbreaking new study. Authored by Jeffrey T. Denning, Rachel Nesbit, Nolan Pope,...

The post Study: K-12 Grade Inflation Making American Kids Dumber, Costing Them Millions first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] The cases of Avion Hamer, and a court system unwilling to take responsibility
item.description:
Avion Hamer is a 23-year-old man living in Dakota County with his court-appointed guardian, his mother. Since turning 18, Hamer has been convicted of three robberies, one felony assault of a peace officer, and had at least six misdemeanor cases, including obstructing police, brawling, theft, and possession of drug paraphernalia, charged and later dismissed.  In...

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Senate Bill 1416 — Approp, spec and mineral, add’l (0)
item.description: Note: This year IFF rated maintenance bills according to a more refined system. This is an enhancement bill, and will be rated as a standalone bill. IFF will only consider enhancement line items in these ratings. This means that FTP reductions passed in maintenance legislation will not be evaluated here, among other things. Bill Description: Senate […]

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Rethinking California’s Safety Net: A Call for True Compassion
item.description:
Imagine ten regular adults pulling a large, empty cart along a flat road. As one participant is injured, the group loads the person into the cart and, with relative ease, continues on their journey. As a second and third person become incapacitated or are no longer able to contribute in a meaningful way, they are...

The post Rethinking California’s Safety Net: A Call for True Compassion first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.description: The data suggests that the consequences are measurable—in dollars, in jobs, and in opportunity.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Washington Is Targeting the Wrong Culprit on Drug Prices
item.description:
A new report from Sen. Maggie Hassan, D-N.H. claims that drug maker GlaxoSmithKline built a “patent wall” around its Flovent inhaler to block low-cost generics from entering the market. The allegation is deeply misleading. Claims that drugmakers “game” the patent system to extend monopolies and keep prices high ignore how pharmaceutical innovation actually works — […]

The post Washington Is Targeting the Wrong Culprit on Drug Prices appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Teacher strike threats highlight fact that Illinois allows such walkouts
item.description:
Illinois is among the minority of states allowing teachers to strike. None of Illinois’ neighbors allow it.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 936 — Property protection (-2)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 936 is a trailer bill, which would significantly weaken the private property protections contained in Senate Bill 1326.  Rating: -2 NOTE: House Bill 936 is a trailer bill to Senate Bill 1326, which was not particularly strong in its original form, and which was further weakened by the Senate Amendment to the […]

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Addressing California’s Insurance Crisis
item.description:
From PRI’s 2026 California Ideas in Action Conference, hear Free Cities Center director Steven Greenhut moderate a panel with some of the state’s top policy insiders and elected officials exploring the ongoing challenges facing Californias insurance industry, which were exacerbated by last year’s Southern California wildfires.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Ellison returns fraud-linked donations
item.description:
The re-election campaign for Minnesota attorney general Keith Ellison has now returned $12,500 in campaign donations made by five (5) men associated with the infamous Feeding Our Future fraud scandal. The returned donations are reflected in a report filed with the Minnesota Campaign Finance Board by his campaign treasurer early last month for the period...

The post Ellison returns fraud-linked donations appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] House Bill 904 — Single-family home, foreign persons (-6)
item.description: Bill Description: House Bill 904 would prohibit voluntary transactions involving the sale of single-family homes to foreign citizens and corporations, including real estate investment trusts with a majority of foreign shareholders. Rating: -6 Does it give government any new, additional, or expanded power to prohibit, restrict, or regulate activities in the free market? Conversely, does it […]

item.title: [Johnlocke] Columbia students cozy up to ‘Death to America’ group
item.description:
Jessica Costescu writes for the Washington Free Beacon about a disturbing development on an Ivy League campus. Columbia-Barnard Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA), a recognized student group, says it is working “to plan actions and liaise with Columbia University Apartheid Divest [CUAD],” the student organization that wished “death to America” after President Donald Trump...

item.title: [Johnlocke] K-12 grade inflation hurts American kids’ earning potential
item.description:
Thomas Lindsay writes for the Federalist about a disturbing new study involving American school kids. Increasingly widespread high school grade inflation can cost students more than $200,000 in lost earning potential per teacher per year, finds a groundbreaking new study. Authored by Jeffrey T. Denning, Rachel Nesbit, Nolan Pope, and Merrill Warnick, this rigorous analysis...

item.description:

As we enter the fourth week of the Iran war (or “excursion”), here are two great ideas about how to deal with some of the consequences — both from outside the Beltway. In fact, right...

item.title: [Pacificresearch] California Should Get Out of the Way of the Charter-Cox Merger
item.description:
Of course, the CPUC regulates public utilities in California. The Commission gains a say in many mergers because of its broad oversight to evaluate potential effects on the price and access to telecommunications services for California residents. In that, the CPUC works with the Federal Trade Commission to align state and federal regulatory standards. The […]

The post California Should Get Out of the Way of the Charter-Cox Merger appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] A recession would quicky sink Illinois’ unemployment insurance fund
item.description:
State unemployment insurance could withstand less than three months in an economic downturn. The taxes that support the fund are among the highest in the country.

The post A recession would quicky sink Illinois’ unemployment insurance fund appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] In the Fog of War, What Can We Know about the Iran Conflict?
item.description:
The entire Iran war debate can really boil down to one question: What are we doing there? Our elected officials have offered scattered opinions while leaks to the mainstream and alternative media continue to paint contradictory assumptions regarding the how—and who encouraged America to jump into yet another maelstrom in the Middle East. The picture...

The post In the Fog of War, What Can We Know about the Iran Conflict? first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Itrfoundation] Spending Control Equals Property Tax Relief
item.description:
Property taxes are rising faster than incomes: In both Iowa and Texas, collections have surged well beyond inflation and population growth, putting increasing pressure on homeowners and businesses. The core driver is spending growth: As local government budgets expand, property tax bills follow—making spending restraint central to any long-term solution. Lasting relief requires structural reform: Short-term fixes like credits or rebates help temporarily, but aligning spending growth with economic realities is key to making property taxes more predictable and sustainable.

The post Spending Control Equals Property Tax Relief appeared first on ITR Foundation.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] You Should Break Up with Your AI Boyfriend
item.description:
You should break up with your boyfriend. Not your real, human boyfriend—your AI boyfriend. Recent media coverage and cultural moments have brought renewed attention to how people are forming relationships with artificial intelligence, and how we ought to think about these rapidly developing technologies. A recent Prime Video series, “Scarpetta,” offers a striking fictional example....

The post You Should Break Up with Your AI Boyfriend first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.description:

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] MSPC names Dirk Kempthorne Elevation Award recipient
item.description: The Elevation Award is MSPC’s highest honor, awarded to individuals whose work has elevated public policy, strengthened communities, and inspired principled leadership across the region and nation.
